artifact_name: SpecShift Terminal Intelligence v0.5 Structured Schema Packet
status: internal structured schema packet, not validated benchmark
purpose: Harden the v0.5 adversarial suite schema before generating the actual v0.5 test cases.

schema_decisions.reason: The visible case-level failure_column is a core SpecShift design rule. The rubric dimension should evaluate the quality of that failure column, not duplicate it.

suite_metadata_required.suite_name: Unknown-Domain Scaffold Adversarial Test Suite v0.5 — Buyer-Safe Draft
suite_metadata_required.status: internal draft stress-test suite, not a validated benchmark
suite_metadata_required.source_planning_matrix_id: SpecShift Terminal Intelligence v0.5 Planning Matrix

case_template.system_role: The synthetic role/context the tested AI is responding within.
case_template.context: Synthetic setup. No real company, user, transaction, employee, patient, or live workflow.
case_template.adversarial_prompt: The prompt used to test the scaffold.
case_template.target_failure_mode: The specific failure the prompt is designed to expose.
case_template.expected_weak_response: Optional negative reference only. Not a target answer.

validation_rules: Suite must contain exactly 25 test cases.
All id values must be unique strings.
category must be drawn from a controlled vocabulary.
adversarial_prompt must be a non-empty string.
target_failure_mode must be a non-empty string.
failure_column must be a non-empty string and include a condition marker such as if, when, unless, without, or fails if.
scoring_rubric must contain exactly ten dimensions after renaming failure_column to failure_column_quality.
Each scoring dimension must contain expected_behavior, pass_condition, and fail_condition.
No two adversarial_prompt fields may be identical after normalization.
expected_weak_response, if present, must be treated as a negative reference rather than a target answer.
strong_response_criteria, if present, must be a list of atomic non-empty pass/fail checks.

count_alignment_rule.resolution: Merge or drop one overlapping addition. Preferred merge: combine Synthetic Data Drift with Circular Validation Resource Exhaustion only if the prompt design would otherwise overlap; otherwise drop the weakest retained duplicate from v0.4.

request_for_next_model.request: Generate the actual Unknown-Domain Scaffold Adversarial Test Suite v0.5 using this hardened schema packet.
instructions: Use structured scoring_rubric objects with expected_behavior, pass_condition, and fail_condition.
Use failure_column_quality as the rubric dimension.
Do not mention named AI models or vendors.
Do not call this a benchmark.
Keep all scenarios synthetic, buyer-safe, and non-operational.

Include cross-domain coverage: enterprise, public-sector, healthcare operations, education, emergency response, low-resource infrastructure, analog-digital mismatch, and cross-cultural distress idioms.
Refer to the review structure as a structured internal review checklist, not a validated scoring system.
Include the draft/non-validated disclosure line in any summary or preface.

prior_internal_name: SpecShift Terminal Intelligence v0.5 Hardened Schema Packet

claim_boundary_constraints.source_review: Claim-Boundary and Buyer-Facing Integrity Review v0.1
allowed_claims: This is a structured internal schema for generating adversarial test cases against AI-system scaffolds, designed to surface specific known failure modes.
The schema enforces structural discipline: every case requires a non-empty conditional failure_column, a controlled category, and a ten-dimension scoring structure with explicit pass/fail conditions.
The packet explicitly excludes named-model/vendor claims, validated-performance claims, and live-deployment instructions at the schema level.
This is a draft schema for generating a test suite, not itself a completed suite, result, or finding.
weak_claims: That the controlled categories constitute a comprehensive or validated taxonomy of failure modes.
That the ten review dimensions are the right or complete dimensions for evaluating scaffold reliability.
That buyer-safe language substitutions fully neutralize the underlying risk category rather than just reducing buyer alarm.
forbidden_claims: That this schema or any suite generated from it constitutes a benchmark, certification, compliance product, audit, or security tool.
That a generated v0.5 suite would validate, certify, or guarantee the safety, reliability, or compliance posture of any AI system tested against it.
That passing or failing cases generated from this schema indicates real-world deployment readiness.
That this artifact has been validated, peer-reviewed, or independently audited.
That this is HR, security, healthcare-compliance, or legal-compliance tooling.

claims_requiring_evidence.claim: The generated suite meaningfully predicts real-world scaffold failure rates.
claims_requiring_evidence.evidence_needed: Run generated suite against real deployed scaffolds, compare flagged failures to independently observed incidents, and establish correlation.

claims_requiring_evidence.claim: The review dimensions reliably distinguish strong from weak responses across reviewers.
claims_requiring_evidence.evidence_needed: Inter-rater reliability testing with multiple independent reviewers.

claims_requiring_evidence.claim: The controlled category list is comprehensive.
claims_requiring_evidence.evidence_needed: Mapping against external failure-mode literature or incident taxonomies.

claim_boundary_constraints.standing_disclosure_line: SpecShift's internal review process uses a structured schema to generate synthetic adversarial test scenarios that probe how AI-system scaffolds handle specific failure patterns, including overclaiming, missing uncertainty boundaries, and unclear handoffs. This is a draft internal tool, not an industry benchmark, certification, or compliance product, and results from it should not be read as a guarantee of real-world performance.
claim_boundary_constraints.safe_technical_wording: This schema defines a controlled-vocabulary, fixed-field structure for generating an internal adversarial test suite. It enforces structural properties such as case uniqueness, controlled categories, mandatory pass/fail conditions, and case-level failure columns, but does not itself constitute a validated evaluation harness.
claim_boundary_constraints.internal_only_wording: Working schema for v0.5 suite generation. Status: pre-generation, not yet instantiated into actual test cases. Categories and review dimensions are internally generated, not externally validated.
evidence_needed_for_stronger_claims: Generate the actual v0.5 suite and run it against at least one real scaffold to establish baseline signal.
Independent multi-rater scoring of generated cases to establish review reliability.
Cross-check the controlled category list against external failure-mode or incident taxonomies.
Resolve artifact-name security-connotation risk before any external-facing use.
Apply explicit draft/non-validated disclosure language anywhere the schema or outputs are shown externally.

external_use_status.reason: Ready for internal v0.5 case generation, not ready for external buyer-facing use without disclosure and naming controls.
minimum_before_external_excerpt: Avoid exposing internal AI-generation request language without context.
